title: Data structure
text: Start.   writing script follow along, use packages article: Load data tables.   Data tables can loaded individually collectively needed.
code: library(midfielddata) library(data.table) # Load one table as needed data(student)  # Or load multiple tables data(course, term, degree)

title: Data structure
text: list variables dataset provide overview information available. Keys composite keys data table indicated. student ID variable mcid primary key linking four tables.
code: student          Variable     Class Keys 1            mcid character  key 2            race character      3             sex character      4     institution character      5        transfer character      6  hours_transfer   numeric      7        age_desc character      8      us_citizen character      9        home_zip character      10    high_school character      11       sat_math   numeric      12     sat_verbal   numeric      13       act_comp   numeric       term               Variable     Class Keys 1                 mcid character  key 2                 term character  key 3                 cip6 character      4          institution character      5                level character      6             standing character      7                 coop character      8           hours_term   numeric      9   hours_term_attempt   numeric      10         hours_cumul   numeric      11 hours_cumul_attempt   numeric      12            gpa_term   numeric      13           gpa_cumul   numeric       course               Variable     Class Keys 1                 mcid character  key 2          term_course character  key 3               abbrev character  key 4               number character  key 5          institution character      6               course character      7              section character      8                 type character      9         faculty_rank character      10        hours_course   numeric      11               grade character      12 discipline_midfield character       degree      Variable     Class Keys 1        mcid character  key 2 term_degree character  key 3        cip6 character  key 4 institution character      5      degree character

title: Data structure
text: Student-level demographic information 97,555 degree-seeking undergraduate students. Data “student-level” refers information collected undergraduate institutions individual students matriculation, example, age, sex, race/ethnicity. student data table 97,555 observations 13 variables structured row-record form, , information associated particular ID occupies single row—one record per student. data dictionary documented ?student. Student IDs institution names anonymized remove identifiable information. Counts category. Race/ethnicity sex often used grouping variables.
code: student #>                  mcid          race    sex   institution              transfer #>                <char>        <char> <char>        <char>                <char> #>     1: MCID3111142225         Asian   Male Institution B   First-Time Transfer #>     2: MCID3111142283         Asian Female Institution J   First-Time Transfer #>     3: MCID3111142290         Asian   Male Institution J   First-Time Transfer #>    ---                                                                         #> 97553: MCID3112898894         White Female Institution B First-Time in College #> 97554: MCID3112898895         White Female Institution B First-Time in College #> 97555: MCID3112898940 Other/Unknown   Male Institution B First-Time in College #>        hours_transfer age_desc us_citizen home_zip high_school sat_math #>                 <num>   <char>     <char>   <char>      <char>    <num> #>     1:             NA Under 25        Yes     <NA>        <NA>       NA #>     2:             NA Under 25        Yes    22020        <NA>      560 #>     3:             NA Under 25        Yes    23233      471872      510 #>    ---                                                                  #> 97553:             NA Under 25        Yes    53716      501160      510 #> 97554:             NA Under 25        Yes    53029      500853      420 #> 97555:             NA Under 25        Yes    20016      090073      470 #>        sat_verbal act_comp #>             <num>    <num> #>     1:         NA       NA #>     2:        230       NA #>     3:        380       NA #>    ---                     #> 97553:        590       24 #> 97554:        590       32 #> 97555:        540       32 # Anonymized ID sample sample(student$mcid, 8) #> [1] "MCID3111312879" "MCID3112197606" "MCID3111735491" "MCID3112243871" #> [5] "MCID3112849521" "MCID3112669477" "MCID3111754188" "MCID3112290710"  # Anonymized institutions sort(unique(student$institution)) #> [1] "Institution B" "Institution C" "Institution J" # N by institution student[, .N, by = "institution"][order(-N)] #>      institution     N #>           <char> <int> #> 1: Institution B 45660 #> 2: Institution C 26712 #> 3: Institution J 25183  # N by race/ethnicity student[, .N, by = "race"][order(-N)] #>               race     N #>             <char> <int> #> 1:           White 73850 #> 2:   International  7354 #> 3:        Hispanic  5386 #> 4:   Other/Unknown  4509 #> 5:           Asian  4193 #> 6:           Black  1860 #> 7: Native American   403  # title: Data structure
text: Student-level term information 97,555 undergraduates including program code, academic standing, grade point average individual students term. term data table 639,915 observations 13 variables structured block-record form, , records associated particular ID can span multiple rows—one record per term every student. data dictionary documented ?term. Terms encoded YYYYT, YYYY year start academic year, T encodes semester quarter—Fall (1), Winter (2), Spring (3), Summer (4, 5, 6)—within academic year example, academic year 1995–96, 19951 encodes Fall 95–96 (calendar year 1995) 19953 encodes Spring 95–96 (calendar year 1996) 19954 encodes Summer 95–96, first session (calendar year 1996) Different institutions supply data different time spans. Programs encoded cip6 variable, 6-digit character based 2010 Classification Instructional Programs (CIP) (NCES 2010). Student level used determining timely completion terms transfer students.
code: term #>                   mcid   term   cip6   institution         level #>                 <char> <char> <char>        <char>        <char> #>      1: MCID3111142225  19881 140901 Institution B 01 First-year #>      2: MCID3111142283  19881 240102 Institution J 01 First-year #>      3: MCID3111142283  19883 240102 Institution J 01 First-year #>     ---                                                          #> 639913: MCID3112898894  20181 451001 Institution B 01 First-year #> 639914: MCID3112898895  20181 302001 Institution B 01 First-year #> 639915: MCID3112898940  20181 050103 Institution B 01 First-year #>                   standing   coop hours_term hours_term_attempt hours_cumul #>                     <char> <char>      <num>              <num>       <num> #>      1:      Good Standing     No          7                  7           7 #>      2: Academic Probation     No          6                  6           6 #>      3: Academic Probation     No         12                 12          18 #>     ---                                                                     #> 639913:      Good Standing     No         13                 13          13 #> 639914:      Good Standing     No         18                 18          18 #> 639915:      Good Standing     No         15                 15          15 #>         hours_cumul_attempt gpa_term gpa_cumul #>                       <num>    <num>     <num> #>      1:                   7     2.56      2.56 #>      2:                   6     1.85      1.85 #>      3:                  18     1.93      1.90 #>     ---                                        #> 639913:                  13     3.52      3.52 #> 639914:                  18     3.50      3.50 #> 639915:                  15     2.18      2.18 # Range of data by institution x <- term[, .(min_term = min(term), max_term = max(term)), by = "institution"] x[order(min_term, max_term)] #>      institution min_term max_term #>           <char>   <char>   <char> #> 1: Institution J    19881    20096 #> 2: Institution B    19881    20181 #> 3: Institution C    19901    20154 # A sample of cip6 values sort(unique(sample(term$cip6, 8))) #> [1] "230101" "240102" "240199" "260901" "261399" "451101" "511104" "521301" # Possible values sort(unique(term$level)) #> [1] "01 First-year"      "02 Second-year"     "03 Third-year"      #> [4] "04 Fourth-year"     "05 Fifth-year Plus"

title: Data structure
text: Student-level course information 97,555 undergraduates including course name number, credit hours, student grades individual students term course. course data table 3,289,532 observations 12 variables structured block-record form, , records associated particular ID can span multiple rows—one record student course term. data dictionary documented ?course. Sample course names. course names contribute composite key, thus NAs possible. Possible values faculty rank variable. Possible values type course. Sample possible values MIDFIELD-assigned discipline. Using course abbreviation number keys, can count number unique courses data.
code: course #>                    mcid term_course abbrev number   institution #>                  <char>      <char> <char> <char>        <char> #>       1: MCID3111142225       19881   ECEN   2230 Institution B #>       2: MCID3111142225       19881   ECEN   4811 Institution B #>       3: MCID3111142225       19881   MCEN   4147 Institution B #>      ---                                                        #> 3289530: MCID3112898940       20181   JPNS   1010 Institution B #> 3289531: MCID3112898940       20181   MATH   1150 Institution B #> 3289532: MCID3112898940       20181   SOCY   1004 Institution B #>                            course section         type faculty_rank #>                            <char>  <char>       <char>       <char> #>       1:       Microprocessor Lab     005         <NA>         <NA> #>       2:           Neural Signals     001         <NA>         <NA> #>       3:      Engineering Economy     001         <NA>         <NA> #>      ---                                                            #> 3289530:     Beginning Japanese 1     009 Face-to-Face     Lecturer #> 3289531:  Precalculus Mathematics     012 Face-to-Face     Lecturer #> 3289532: Deviance In U S  Society     100 Face-to-Face   Instructor #>          hours_course  grade                  discipline_midfield #>                 <num> <char>                               <char> #>       1:            1      C Engineering: Electrical and Computer #>       2:            3      C Engineering: Electrical and Computer #>       3:            3     B+              Engineering: Mechanical #>      ---                                                          #> 3289530:            5      C    Language and Literature: Japanese #> 3289531:            4     C-                          Mathematics #> 3289532:            3      B           Social Sciences: Sociology # Sample course names sample(course$course, 10) #>  [1] NA                                                           #>  [2] "Psychology of Gender"                                       #>  [3] "Calculus 1"                                                 #>  [4] "Intro Japanese History"                                     #>  [5] "Environmental Design Studio 3"                              #>  [6] "Personal Computing"                                         #>  [7] NA                                                           #>  [8] "Ct-Vascular Func/Exercis"                                   #>  [9] "Introduction To Differential Equations With Linear Algebra" #> [10] "Anth Prspect/Contemp Iss" # Possible values sort(unique(course$faculty_rank)) #>  [1] "Academic Professional"     "Adjunct Faculty"           #>  [3] "Administrative Assistant"  "Assistant Professor"       #>  [5] "Associate Professor"       "Clinical Faculty"          #>  [7] "Graduate Assistant"        "Instructor"                #>  [9] "Lecturer"                  "Non-Academic Professional" #> [11] "Other"                     "Pre/Post Doctoral"         #> [13] "Professor"                 "Research Faculty"          #> [15] "Senior Administrator"      "Technician/Specialist"     #> [17] "Undergraduate Assistant"   "Visiting Faculty" # Sample of possible values sort(sample(unique(course$discipline_midfield), 20)) #>  [1] "Academic Support"                                      #>  [2] "Agriculture: Horticulture"                             #>  [3] "Area Studies: American"                                #>  [4] "Area Studies: Community"                               #>  [5] "Area Studies: Film"                                    #>  [6] "Biological and Biomedical Sciences: Neuroscience"      #>  [7] "Cooperative Education"                                 #>  [8] "Education: Adult"                                      #>  [9] "Education: Physical and Coaching"                      #> [10] "Engineering: Electrical and Computer"                  #> [11] "First-Year"                                            #> [12] "Health Professions: Pharmacy"                          #> [13] "Language and Literature: Chinese"                      #> [14] "Library and Information Sciences"                      #> [15] "Personal and Culinary Services: Restaurant Management" #> [16] "Philosophy"                                            #> [17] "Protective Services: Fire Protection"                  #> [18] "Special Topics"                                        #> [19] "Visual and Performing Arts: Interior Design"           #> [20] "Visual and Performing Arts: Music" # Unique courses x <- unique(course[, .(abbrev, number, institution)]) x #>        abbrev number   institution #>        <char> <char>        <char> #>     1:   ECEN   2230 Institution B #>     2:   ECEN   4811 Institution B #>     3:   MCEN   4147 Institution B #>    ---                             #> 23014:   ENVD   1976 Institution B #> 23015:   ORGN   1001 Institution B #> 23016:   IPHY   2010 Institution B  # N courses by institution x <- x[, .N, by = c("institution")] x[order(-N)] #>      institution     N #>           <char> <int> #> 1: Institution B  8778 #> 2: Institution C  7238 #> 3: Institution J  7000

title: Data structure
text: Student-level degree information 49,543 undergraduates including institution, program, term, baccalaureate degree student. degree data table 49,665 observations 5 variables structured block-record form, , records associated particular ID can span multiple rows—one record student completing program term. Multiple degrees can occur term different terms. data dictionary documented ?degree. dataset contains records graduates , thus degree fewer rows student. term_degree variable indicates student graduated cip6 indicates program. Sample degree names. number unique degrees data program code. Number degrees earned per student. practice data, one earned two degrees; 122 students earned two degrees.
code: degree #>                  mcid term_degree   cip6   institution #>                <char>      <char> <char>        <char> #>     1: MCID3111142225       19881 141001 Institution B #>     2: MCID3111142290       19921 141001 Institution J #>     3: MCID3111142294       19903 141001 Institution J #>    ---                                                 #> 49663: MCID3112839623       20181 160102 Institution B #> 49664: MCID3112845220       20181 270101 Institution B #> 49665: MCID3112845673       20174 090101 Institution B #>                                                          degree #>                                                          <char> #>     1:            Bachelor of Science in Electrical Engineering #>     2:            Bachelor of Science in Electrical Engineering #>     3:            Bachelor of Science in Electrical Engineering #>    ---                                                          #> 49663:                       Bachelor of Science in Linguistics #> 49664:                       Bachelor of Science in Mathematics #> 49665: Bachelor of Science in Speech Communication and Rhetoric sort(sample(degree$degree, 10)) #>  [1] "Bachelor of Arts in Geography"                                        #>  [2] "Bachelor of Arts in Political Science and Government"                 #>  [3] "Bachelor of Arts in Sociology"                                        #>  [4] "Bachelor of Musical Arts"                                             #>  [5] "Bachelor of Science in Biochemistry"                                  #>  [6] "Bachelor of Science in Computer and Informational Sciences"           #>  [7] "Bachelor of Science in Mechanical Engineering"                        #>  [8] "Bachelor of Science in Mechanical Engineering"                        #>  [9] "Bachelor of Science in Wildlife and Wildlands Science and Management" #> [10] "Bachelor of Science in Wildlife and Wildlands Science and Management" # Count the number of unique program codes length(unique(degree$cip6)) #> [1] 153 # Degree count by student N_degrees <- degree[, .(degree_count = .N), by = "mcid"]  # Number of students by the degree count N_degrees[, .(N_students = .N), by = "degree_count"] #>    degree_count N_students #>           <int>      <int> #> 1:            1      49421 #> 2:            2        122

title: Data linked by student ID
text: Start.   writing script follow along, use packages article: Load data tables.   Data tables can loaded individually collectively needed. View data dictionaries help pages using ?student, ?course, ?term, ?degree.
code: library(midfielddata) library(data.table)  # data.table printout option options(datatable.print.nrows = 70) # Load one table as needed data(student)  # Or load multiple tables data(course, term, degree)

what: Student 1: Degree in psychology and sociology
title: Data linked by student ID
text: display records one specific student, using ID subset data table. student.   Yields one row per student. course.   student, course records span 47 rows, one row per course. omit several columns rename others, obtain readable output. Term.   , records span 10 rows, one row per term. , can editing improve readability, assuming variables might need analysis. Degree.   example, records span 2 rows, one row per degree. degrees earned term, Spring 2009.

text: Degree.   example, student earned two degrees separate times. Usually interested student records time first degree, ignoring subsequent degrees (unless degrees relevant one’s study). Thus, extracting course term information, filter terms later first degree term—case, term 19961. student.   expected, yields one row. course.   student, records show 52 courses (one row per course) leading first degree. Term.   , records show 12 terms (one row per term) leading first degree.

title: MIDFIELD Data Sample
text: Contains anonymized student-level records 98,000 undergraduates three US institutions 1988 2018 organized four data tables keyed student ID. Student-level records collected undergraduate institutions individual students including demographics, programs academic standing term, individual courses, grades, degrees. data proportionate stratified sample MIDFIELD (Ohland 2023), database managed since 2023 American Society Engineering Education. midfielddata datasets suitable practice working student-level records using midfieldr package (Layton et al. 2026) , however, suitable drawing inferences program attributes student experiences—midfielddata practice, research.

title: MIDFIELD Data Sample
text: Install MIDFIELDR drat repository :
code: install.packages("midfielddata",   repos = "https://MIDFIELDR.github.io/drat/",   type = "source" )

title: MIDFIELD Data Sample
text: Data tables can loaded individually collectively needed. Tutorials: examine data greater detail package articles. one uses data developed detail midfieldr website.
code: library(midfielddata)  # Load one table as needed data(student)  # Or load multiple tables data(term, course, degree)

title: Student-level course data — course
text: Student-level course information approximately 98,000 undergraduates, keyed student ID. Data "student-level" refers information collected undergraduate institutions individual students, example, course name number, credit hours, student grades.

title: Student-level course data — course
code: data(course)

title: Student-level course data — course
text: data.frame data.table 12 variables approximately 3.3M observations 97,555 unique students occupying 325 MB memory: mcid Character, de-identified student ID. Key column. term Character, academic year term, format YYYYT. Key column. abbrev Character, course alphabetical identifier, e.g. ENGR, MATH, ENGL. Key column. number Character, course numeric identifier, e.g. 101, 3429. Key column. institution Character, de-identified institution name, e.g., Institution , Institution B, etc. course Character, course name, e.g., Astrophysics III, Calculus Social Science Business, Corp Financial Rprtng 1, Environmental Sanitation II, Fitness Wellness, Introductory Astronomy 2, Changing Environment, etc. section Character, course section identifier, one four characters, e.g., 1, 2, 01, 14, 001, 040, 785, H02, R01, 300E, 888R, etc. type Character, predominant delivery method section, e.g., Blended, Distance Education, Face--Face, Online, etc. faculty_rank Character, academic rank person teaching course, e.g., Assistant Professor, Associate Professor, Graduate Assistant, Visiting Faculty, etc. hours_course Numeric, number credit-hours successful course completion. grade Character, course grade, e.g., +, , -, B+, , NG, etc. discipline_midfield Character, variable grouping courses academic discipline assigned pre-2023 MIDFIELD data curator, e.g., Anthropology, Business, Computer Science, Engineering, Language Literature, Mathematics,Visual Performing Arts, etc.

title: Student-level course data — course
text: 2022 MIDFIELD database

title: Student-level course data — course
text: Course data structured block-record form, , records associated particular ID can span multiple rows—one record per student per course per term. Terms encoded YYYYT, YYYY year start academic year T encodes semester quarter within academic year Fall (1), Winter (2), Spring (3), Summer (4, 5, 6). example, academic year 1995–96, Fall 95–96 encoded 19951, Spring 95–96 encoded 19953, first Summer 95-96 term encoded 19954. source database includes special month-long sessions encoded letters , B, C, etc., though none included sample. possible values grade variable includes NG "grade given", W indicating withdrawal. example, lab course may mandatory co-requisite lecture course, grade given lecture course lab grade recorded NG. data midfielddata proportionate stratified sample MIDFIELD database, suitable drawing inferences program attributes student experiences—midfielddata provides practice data, research data.

title: Student-level course data — course
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{  # Load data data(course)  # Select specific rows and columns rows_we_want <- course$mcid == MCID3112192438 cols_we_want <- c(mcid, term_course, course, grade)  # View observations for this ID course[rows_we_want, cols_we_want] } # }

title: Student-level degree data — degree
text: Student-level degree information approximately 50,000 undergraduates earning degrees, keyed student ID. Data "student-level" refers information collected undergraduate institutions individual students, example, institution, program, term, baccalaureate degree.

title: Student-level degree data — degree
code: data(degree)

title: Student-level degree data — degree
text: data.frame data.table 5 variables 49,543 observations unique students occupying 6 MB memory: mcid Character, de-identified student ID. Key column. term_degree Character, academic year term student completes program, format YYYYT. cip6 Character, 6-digit CIP code program student enrolled term. Key column. institution Character, de-identified institution name, e.g., Institution , Institution B, etc. degree Character, type degree awarded, e.g., Bachelor Arts Geography, Bachelor Science Finance, etc.

title: Student-level degree data — degree
text: 2022 MIDFIELD database

title: Student-level degree data — degree
text: Degree data structured block-record form, , records associated particular ID can span multiple rows—one record per student per degree. Multiple degrees can occur term different terms. Terms encoded YYYYT, YYYY year start academic year T encodes semester quarter within academic year Fall (1), Winter (2), Spring (3), Summer (4, 5, 6). example, academic year 1995–96, Fall 95–96 encoded 19951, Spring 95–96 encoded 19953, first Summer 95-96 term encoded 19954. source database includes special month-long sessions encoded letters , B, C, etc., though none included sample. program codes, midfielddata uses 2010 version Classification Instructional Programs (CIP). midfieldr package installed loaded, type ?cip details. data midfielddata proportionate stratified sample MIDFIELD database, suitable drawing inferences program attributes student experiences—midfielddata provides practice data, research data.

title: Student-level degree data — degree
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{  # Load data data(degree)  # Select specific rows and columns rows_we_want <- degree$mcid == MCID3112192438 cols_we_want <- c(mcid, term_degree, cip6, degree)  # View observations for this ID degree[rows_we_want, cols_we_want] } # }

title: midfielddata: MIDFIELD Data Sample — midfielddata-package
text: Contains anonymized student-level records 98,000 undergraduates three US institutions 1988 2018 organized four data tables keyed student ID. Student-level records collected undergraduate institutions individual students including demographics, programs academic standing term, individual courses, grades, degrees. 'midfielddata' provides data sets suitable learning practicing basic data skills manipulating student-level records developed 'midfieldr' package. 'midfielddata' also serves database model structuring registrar's data general. data 'midfielddata' , however, suitable drawing inferences program attributes student experiences—'midfielddata' practice, research. development 'midfieldr' 'midfielddata' supported US National Science Foundation grant numbers 1545667 2142087.

title: midfielddata: MIDFIELD Data Sample — midfielddata-package
text: Maintainer: Richard Layton graphdoctor@gmail.com [copyright holder] Authors: Richard Layton graphdoctor@gmail.com [copyright holder] Russell Long [copyright holder] Matthew Ohland ohland@purdue.edu [copyright holder] Marisa Orr [copyright holder] Susan Lord [copyright holder] contributors: US National Science Foundation [funder]

title: Student-level demographic data — student
text: Student-level demographic information approximately 98,000 degree-seeking undergraduate students, keyed student ID. Data "student-level" refers information collected undergraduate institutions individual students, example, age, sex, race/ethnicity matriculation.

title: Student-level demographic data — student
code: data(student)

title: Student-level demographic data — student
text: data.frame data.table 13 variables 97,555 observations unique students occupying 18 MB memory: mcid Character, de-identified student ID. Key column. race Character, race/ethnicity self-reported student, e.g., Asian, Black, Hispanic, etc. sex Character, sex self-reported student, possible values Female, Male, Unknown. institution Character, de-identified institution name, e.g., Institution , Institution B, etc. transfer Character, transfer status, possible values First-Time College, First-Time Transfer. hours_transfer Numeric, number credit hours transferred (NA). age_desc Character, age group, possible values 25 Older, 25. us_citizen Character, US citizenship, possible values , Yes. home_zip Character, home ZIP code (NA), e.g., 02056, 20170, 51301, 80129, etc. high_school Character, code last high school attended admission (NA), e.g., 060075, 210512, 431800, 502195, etc. sat_math Numeric, SAT mathematics test score (NA). sat_verbal Numeric, SAT reading test score (NA). act_comp Numeric, ACT composite test score (NA).

title: Student-level demographic data — student
text: 2022 MIDFIELD database

title: Student-level demographic data — student
text: Student data structured row-record form, , information associated particular ID occupies single row—one record per student. data midfielddata proportionate stratified sample MIDFIELD database, suitable drawing inferences program attributes student experiences—midfielddata provides practice data, research data.

title: Student-level demographic data — student
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{  # Load data data(student)  # Select specific rows and columns rows_we_want <- student$mcid == MCID3112192438 cols_we_want <- c(mcid, institution, transfer, race, sex, age_desc)  # View observations for this ID student[rows_we_want, cols_we_want] } # }

title: Student-level term data — term
text: Student-level term information approximately 98,000 undergraduates, keyed student ID. Data "student-level" refers information collected undergraduate institutions individual students, example, program code, academic standing, grade point average term.

title: Student-level term data — term
code: data(term)

title: Student-level term data — term
text: data.frame data.table 13 variables approximately 640,000 observations 97,555 unique students occupying 73 MB memory: mcid Character, de-identified student ID. Key column. term Character, academic year term, format YYYYT. Key column. cip6 Character, 6-digit CIP code program student enrolled term, e.g., 090101, 141201, 260901, 420101, etc. institution Character, de-identified institution name, e.g., Institution , Institution B, etc. level Character, 01 Freshman, 02 Sophomore, etc. equivalent values current practice data 01 First-Year, 02-Second Year, etc. standing Character, academic standing, e.g., Good Standing, Academic Warning, etc. coop Character, cooperative education term, possible values Yes, . hours_term Numeric, credit hours earned term. hours_term_attempt Numeric, credit hours attempted term. hours_cumul Numeric, cumulative credit hours earned. hours_cumul_attempt Numeric, cumulative credit hours attempted. gpa_term Numeric, term grade point average. gpa_cumul Numeric, cumulative grade point average.

title: Student-level term data — term
text: 2022 MIDFIELD database

title: Student-level term data — term
text: Term data structured block-record form, , records associated particular ID can span multiple rows—one record per student per term. Terms encoded YYYYT, YYYY year start academic year T encodes semester quarter within academic year Fall (1), Winter (2), Spring (3), Summer (4, 5, 6). example, academic year 1995–96, Fall 95–96 encoded 19951, Spring 95–96 encoded 19953, first Summer 95-96 term encoded 19954. source database includes special month-long sessions encoded letters , B, C, etc., though none included sample. program codes, midfielddata uses 2010 version Classification Instructional Programs (CIP). midfieldr package installed loaded, type ?cip details. data midfielddata proportionate stratified sample MIDFIELD database, suitable drawing inferences program attributes student experiences—midfielddata provides practice data, research data.

title: Student-level term data — term
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{  # Load data data(term)  # Select specific rows and columns rows_we_want <- term$mcid == "MCID3112192438" cols_we_want <- c("mcid", "term", "cip6", "level", "standing", "gpa_cumul")  # View observations for this ID term[rows_we_want, cols_we_want] } # }
